Hey everyone, this is Russell Brunson. I want to welcome you to this month's training. And this training I'm excited for, because it's something that every time I talk about this, I don't think people believe me at first. And I usually get some people that fight me against it. And then those who actually do it have amazing success. In fact, I fought this for a long time. And I still struggle to do it consistently. It's something that takes some dedication to do consistently. But it's also where I've seen some of the highest revenues coming from in our company. It's important to do and to be consistent with. And it's what we call our daily sign filled emails. And if you read the dot com secrets book, I talked about this. And it's fun, I had a chance a little while ago. One of the guys who read the book, his name is Tim Schmidt. He read the book, loved it. He actually hired us, paid us $100,000 to come out and train his company. And one of the things that made me laugh is he kind of pulled me inside and said, hey, this is the deal. He's like, right now in our company, every Friday, we send out this big, like, corporate-y style recap email with a bunch of articles and links. And we spend a ton of time and effort doing it. And he's like, I hate it. It doesn't make any money. He's like, I need you to convince everybody that we need to do sign filled emails in our company. I was like, OK. He's hiring me to come in and be the bad guy and convince his team that they need to change everything. But anyway, I was like, all right. So we go through and I teach this whole, I taught like for an hour or two of them on the concept of daily sign filled emails. I showed examples, kind of got them understanding it. And I could tell there was this resistance from his team. They did not want to do it. They fought me tooth and nail. And they were kind of the excuse after excuse. I said, stop. Let's do something really fun. I want all you guys are now to spend three minutes and sit down and write a daily sign filled email about what you guys do, about something happened cool in your life. And then we're going to go around the room, and we're going to actually have everyone share their email. And at first they were kind of like, you know, kind of fighting it. And then they all sat down and they kind of did it really quick. And the structure of an email of a daily sign filled email really quickly is you're basically, you're picking a story that happened to you that day. And it can be anything. That's why we call them sign filled emails. That's like the movie or the TV show sign filled. Right? Like all of these stories are stories about nothing. That's the whole point of them. Right? If you think about the movie, the TV show sign filled, it's a show about nothing. That was the whole funny thing in the show. If you remember one of the episodes when they were pitching their show to NBC called the Jerry show. And they're like, what's the show going to be about? It's like it's a show about nothing. Like, well, why is anyone going to watch it? Well, because it's on TV. You know, and it's just a whole funny thing about how it's a show about nothing. And that's kind of what daily sign filled emails are. You know, we found that as much as we think that our audience wants to be, get content to be educated, for the most part, those who assume that are wrong. What your audience wants is they want to be entertained. Like when all is said and done, that's it. Okay? And it's hard and promise you that you're probably thinking that I'm wrong. You're fighting me or whatever. But when all is said and done, like that's it. They want to be entertained. And so it's entertainment wrapped with, it's content wrapped in entertainment. And that's what these sign filled emails do. It's the structure of it. It's usually you're telling a story about something that's happened to you during the day and somehow you have to weave that back to your offer. I'm going to show you guys a bunch of examples here in a little bit. But that's kind of the concept. And so I had these guys go around and they each sat down and they spent three or four minutes writing one. And I said, okay, let's do them live. And so went and had the first person go. And we had, I think a group of about 22 people. And each person went and told a daily sign filled email and then, and tied it back to some promotion or something they were doing in the company. And they went through and it was like by far the most fun thing of our workshop. People were laughing and having fun and everything. And at the end of it, Tim stood up and said, I'm curious, how many in this room have ever read our Friday emails? And nobody, there's one person, the person who wrote them, rose their hand. Nobody else in the company even read them. And said, how many of you guys would read these emails? And everyone's hands went up. And said, that's the difference. That's how we get people to open and to consume and to read. The reason why people will sit there and I always use the example of Oprah, even though her show's not online any longer. But people would back in the day, they'd watch Oprah five days a week for an hour a day because of this, because she was entertaining. And that's what gets people to open your emails and to keep opening your emails is that. If you're just giving content, you will bore them and they will stop. I don't care how exciting your topic is, how exciting they are. That's not what gets people to stay. People come in for the content and they stay for the relationship with the attractive character. That's kind of how it works. And so that's what's important to understand. So these daily sign fill emails give you a way and give you the ability to communicate with your audience. So I'm gonna go through this graph and we'll talk about a whole bunch of different things here in a minute. But I created this so you can look at this, cuz it's kind of hard to come up. What's a good idea? What's a new thing I can do? And so I made this graph to kind of give you an ability to quickly recall these different ideas and topics and things. And I'll break that down here in a little bit. First I want to show you guys a bunch of different examples of daily sign fill emails. Now to begin with, I'm gonna give credit where credit is due. The person that I initially learned this concept from was a guy named Ben Settle. And I got on Ben Settle's newsletter. This other guy promoted it, I got on newsletter and at first, it kind of drove me crazy, I kept getting these emails for this guy's product and at first, I didn't like it. But I never unsubscribed, I read every single email that he sent and I get so frustrated and so angry. And then one day I remember thinking, man, this guy is like really emotionally like getting me engaged with his emails. That's not something that happens very often. And I was like, I gotta step back and not be frustrated by what he was saying, but respect the process of how he was saying it, what he was doing to cause such an emotional reaction. And after that, I ended up buying his course, these sales, and become great friends with him and I really respect him a ton as a marketer and as a mentor and just a really super cool guy. But that was kind of my first introduction to this. And what's interesting about him is he is the most consistent person I've ever seen. He sends email a day, every single day, for forever. And it's like literally amazing. And he archives my blog, so I'll show you guys can see like, I don't know how many years of these things in the past. But he is one of the best and he only really has one product to sell. He sells it every single day. And that's what's really cool with me is most of us are like, we send three emails or a list about a product and we move on to the next thing. We're so scared to keep doing it where he's been selling the exact same product every day for as long. I've been on his list for three or four years now and I know he's been doing it for before I ever got involved. And so he just is very consistent with one message, one product, one focus. So I'm actually going to start there with him. In fact, if you go here by email, I searched Ben Settle, you can see that like, here's like a million emails from me. Like that's how consistent he is. Because, and I quote, video sales letters always out convert tech sales letters. Really? Is that right? And by the way, I actually agree with Ben at this point. We've had more success recently with tech sales letters and video sales letters. So he is actually right, by the way. Anyway, I think not. Yes, via sales can multiply yourselves through the roof and many do, but anyone saying they are always better is either lying to you and, or has some kind of agenda or simply ignorant. I got to talking to the guy who runs a business that sells the book and they told me that sales letters, some seven years later, was always chugging along, always kicking butt, nearly unchanged and has racked a multi-million dollar sales count over the years. Naturally my team is always trying to make it convert better. One of the things we've done is test the VSL against it. The result, my plain Jane tech sales letter, handily beat the VSL and that's just one of many examples. All of the VSL Nazis out there, quit being dorks. There's no one size fits all anything. And yes, that's free advice. More, this is the beauty of email. I found in many cases, if you do email the way I command you to, in fact, it almost doesn't matter what sales letter format you use. In fact, my drogy Jim Yaggy has told me repeatedly he will often use my email methodologies to send people to a plain order form. They still make tons of sales. And when we partner in the weight loss niche, we can never get one format to pull better than the other, personally because emails were doing the bulk of the selling to our warm list. No, I'm not saying forget sales letters. I'm saying email can do a lot of heavy lifting. It's perfect for people who hate writing long offers. We simply suck at it. So here's the first one I'll send you is, um, this is when I sent out, it's a true story. He flushed $20 million down the toilet today. This is the email said, so yesterday we had a guy apply for my inner circle program. I saw his app come through and I was actually really excited because he was in the golf market. Now I'm no golfer, but I've got a lot of friends doing $20 million plus in the golf market online. I saw his product and I knew it was a home run. So the coach who was going to call him back asked me for my opinion on his business before she called him up and I sat down for 10 minutes and pulled up three things. First is three major competitors. By the way, I'm funnel hacking. Do you guys all know by now? The first is three major competitors. The second I showed her every site that was, that they were successfully buying traffic from third, I showed her the top three converting ads for each of his competitors and fourth, I showed her the sales phones that were, that were converting and the main reason why his was not. I then showed her the two media buyers I would use if I were in the golf market, both who can send over a thousand sales a day consistently and armed with this information. She called the guy up. He was a little cocky and rightfully so he'd sold over a hundred thousand units of his product on TV. Yeah. For some reason couldn't figure out how to get this pesky internet, how to figure out how to get this pesky internet thing. She started, she started sharing some of my ideas and then he stopped her. Look, I read 20 books on internet marketing. There's a single thing that Russell could teach me that I don't already know. So she explained, look, you could read a million books on jujitsu, but that's not going to help you in a street fight. I thought that was pretty funny, but what happened next was just sad. He said, well, Russell doesn't know anything about golf and then he hung up. Now, while he was writing about me not knowing anything about golf, I know everything about selling golf stuff online. I've been doing this for over 10 years now and I've personally trained over 2,500 companies in my offices here in Boise, Idaho in those 10 years. I've worked a lot of golf guys and one golf gal. I've worked with people in just every market I can think of except for bowling. I've never had someone teach bowling, teaching bowling come to me, which makes me sad because bowling is my third favorite sport behind wrestling number one and jujitsu number two. Anyway, for about everything else I can think of, I helped to map out a funnel, show them what they were doing wrong, introduce them to my media buyers and show them what sites to buy ads on and what they should be spending to acquire customer in their specific market. I then usually intro them to the gurus I know in those areas. After speaking on Danny Kennedy's stages for six plus years, I got to meet most of the gurus in most industries and it makes it easy to find connections for those people. Those are the things you can't learn in a book. Those are the things that bring to the table for our inner circle people. Now, my goal for them is to not teach them more stuff, it's to make them more money. Can I show you another example? This is one. This one was awesome too. All right. So this one says, Jiu Jitsu is like wrestling for old fat guys and other marketing stuff. So tomorrow I'm fighting in a Jiu Jitsu tournament. For those of you on my list who don't know what Jiu Jitsu is, it's kind of like wrestling for old fat guys, which is great for me because while I still look like I'm 13 years old, I've actually never, I am actually getting a lot older. I'm 34 years old now and fatter. 30 pounds heavier than when I was wrestling. Anyway, I have wanes in a few hours and as of right now, I'm still seven pounds over. Good thing is I'm a wrestler and we have some awesome weight cutting skills. In fact, I just found my weight cutting clothes this morning. Check them out. And there's a picture of me being a dork. Yes, they're a little tight but my three-year-old told me I look like a ninja so they can't be that bad, right? Anyway, in about an hour I'm going to be going to the wrestling room and within 30 to 40 minutes I'll lose all seven pounds. And tomorrow I get to step on them out with a bunch of younger, faster guys whose only goal in life is to choke me out or to break my arm, whichever comes first. I am so excited. So why do I tell you this? Because this week we did well over six figures in sales, not this month, this week. We did it without any product launches, without any affiliates. And while it's pretty much a normal week for us, this week was very special because we also did it without me actually being in the office. Yep, you guessed it. I spent most of the week in a wrestling room getting ready for the tournament this weekend. Yet we still did six figures in sales while I was gone. Would you like to learn how I did it? Would you like to see how to build a business that can run just as good when you're gone as when you're here? Are you ready to take your company to the next level? If so, I've got good news for you. As long as I don't end up in the hospital after a match this weekend, then I'm going to be coming into the office next week. That gives me time to personally work with two more people to help build out their funnel, the same type of funnel we use to pull in six figure weeks like Clockwork. If you're ready to take your game to the next level and create a business that can truly give you time for you so you can do what you love, let's go on the phone and figure out how we can work together. Sounds good? It's still going to apply here. If you're looking for a get rich quick scheme, this isn't it. If you're looking for a work hard and build an awesome company scheme, then I'm your man. I'm off to cut weight. Wish me luck this weekend. Thanks Russell Brunson. P.S. I know that it's not healthy to cut seven pounds in under an hour, so no email me telling me that it's not healthy. I'm pretty sure stepping them out with something 30 pounds heavier is a lot less healthy than me losing seven pounds of water waiting an hour. That's the one. Again, some random thing happening. I'm about to go to the Jiu Jitsu tournament. I just sent out a sign for the email. From that we had like 70 or 80 applications and did over another $100,000 in sales. You guys see how this works? It's picking things that are happening that are relevant to you that are interesting, that are exciting, that your audience, they're listening to you for a reason. It's typically they come in because they want to learn something and then they stay because they want to be entertained by you. When I was learning from Dan Kennedy, I was at one of his events and he had a few steps. He's like, who's been in the Dan Kennedy world for over a year and everyone stood up. Who's been over five years? A couple people sat down. Who's been studying me for over 10 years, over 20 years, over 30 years? People are still in the room. They've been there for over 30 years. There's probably like six guys still standing in the room. He said, you guys, I have not had an original thought for over 30 years. Why do you guys keep coming? He's like, I haven't taught you anything new. The same thing. He keeps using it every single time. He said, this is a lesson. He's like, people come for the content. They stay for the attractive character. They've been coming for 30 years because of the relationship, because of whatever reason, whatever bond they have with the attractive character. That's what keeps them coming. That's what you're trying to hear through emails, you guys. They came in on a trip wire, a free report, a free book, or whatever, but they stay because of your personality, because of your stories, because they can relate to you. That's what these daily signs for the emails are about. I showed you Ben Settles. This is Dagan Smith. He, for like two years, was consistent every single day, and then he's fallen off for a little while. There's tons of theirs. He had this thing for a while. He got Manboob surgery. He was even putting his name Dagan, Manboob Smith. I wanted you to read this one, because this one, I thought was awesome. I read this email, and it was amazing. It's called Buying Happiness. It makes one really big, so you guys can see. All right, so it says, in the shower this morning, I started thinking about that old saying, that money can't buy happiness. I wondered, why would anyone say or believe something like that? Now, I don't think that money itself produces eternal joy or anything like that. After all, look at all the countless tales of despair attached to winners of those mega-millionaire lotteries over the years. Littered amongst them, you'll find heightened levels of greed, gluttony, powerful jealousy, and colors of the rainbow of sin, and they all tend to result in the less positive outcomes. But I do believe you can unequivocally buy happiness. Let me tell you why. I can remember one time, as an awkward and chunky kid, supporting my fresh new high-top fade, walking to assembly about two months into this scholastic year. The walk itself was hilly and probably a little shy of half a mile. We held our Monday morning assemblies in the school gym, which was detached from our main school, so we walked. Rain sleet or snow, we walked. But halfway through the walk, where it started to get really hilly, I could hear Rich, an eighth grader behind me say, Dagan, why are your legs so skinny, but you have boobs like a girl? Having no answer to that question, I felt it sting to the core. I sent it from my head down and continued up the hill. I was a little kiddy man boobs, which I carried with me until the age of, I don't know, say 30. As you can probably imagine, I was always self-conscious about taking my shirt off. Having boobs and being a boy ain't all it's cracked up to be. Now by the age of about 25, I weighed 235 pounds, had a body fat percentage of 7%, and could spend 430 pounds for three sets of six. And guess what? I still had those pesky boobs. So God knows I tried to get rid of those bad boys through discipline sweat. And that's why when you see all those e-books claiming they cure man boobs, I get pissed. It's horse crap. I don't know how to pronounce that word. Is medical condition, it can only be solved by one thing, surgery. So last year, I bought myself a present. Yep, you guessed it, a boob job. I can tell you flat out that it was the best $8,000 I've ever spent. I am happier. Example number two. Anyway, he goes on, so that was the man boob thing, and then he goes on to his mom, grandma, blah, blah, blah, blah, and goes on. Oh, he even talked about me in here. Russell Brunson, I made it in his email. And I think eventually, he writes the longest emails of all time. I think eventually he tries to sell something here at the end. Boom, he sells maximum leverage. So did you see that at work, so? So he's telling a story, gets you engaged, gets you entertained, and somehow he then translates it into his offer. Like, when'd you open that? Like, what is this guy talking about? Hey, it's pretty lonely when you're a full-time home-based business owner, especially if you're a loner or workaholic like me, living on the other side of the world away from the hub of the home business boom in a place where the whole concept of working from home is foreign. Let me tell you a funny story set the stage. Today, so boom, now you're in story time, right? Story time is where people start perking up and start actually listening to you. All right, today I had to go to the FreeZone Business Center, take care of my son's residency visa here. He'd not have to be with me. All he needed was my signature. Actually, my body needed to be with me. Ever since I and I got married, I have literally taken her with me everywhere. We're inseparable. As much as loners I am, I was raised on the value that one's family is the only real friends we have in the world. And I lived that rule as if it were God made. For years, everyone who saw us thought it was cute that I brought my wife everywhere, but they figured the moment we had kids also changed. So far it hasn't. Even to the business center or meeting. I lug along my wife, my child with me. I might send them off to nearby to keep them busy, but they're always with me. A lesser amount would be embarrassed, but I am not. Back to the funny story. There I am in the middle of a bunch of suits in the waiting room. Baby carriage in front of me and my gurgling baby in arms and playing with him. All of a sudden Adam decides it's a great time to push out a big ol' fart. The room was quiet and his fart echoed loud and clear for all the hair. Many turned to look disapprovingly in our direction. My wife was humiliated and she blushed red and prepared to leave the room. Me? I thought it was hilarious. As I've often said to you guys, the thing I value above all else is my independence. I don't care what anyone else thinks. I do things my way and that's that. You should try sometimes to celebrate your independence as liberating. But I also mentioned at start of the message that having a home business is lonely. And as it here comes the transition and as fulfilling as the company of a family can be, there are times when your success depends on being surrounded by your peers. People with the same interests you're working on similar problems you. Over seven plus years I've done online traffic, all of which I made living exclusively for my home business. I found my most productive times when I've had my biggest breakthroughs or while interacting with my peers. I've had a lot of partnerships other times in masterminds. Indeed my first attempt at anything kind of blows. But if I get some feedback from someone else, their ideas and mine turn to something quite magical. For instance, I've been in one mastermind for over five years before launching anything and he goes, anyway, he keeps going in here. Talks about his paid transitions and then talks about his communicationology course. So that's kind of the structure of Daily Signed for the Emails. I want to show you guys a bunch of those so you can kind of see different styles, how Dagen does his, how Ben does his, how I do mine, how Jim does his. You can see some examples because you've got to find your own tone, your own voice. The biggest key is telling a story that's relevant to what's happening in your life right now, but then figuring out how to transition that into one of your offers. If you don't transition it, just waste some of the email. Every email should be pushing somebody somewhere. Otherwise, I don't know. If there's not a link for me then I, yeah. So there you go. All right, so Daily Signed and the email. So here's kind of the graph that you guys, if you're part of the DotCom series monthly, you probably got this in the mail or if you were active when this month happened and so you can print this up or you have this on your wall, if not, you can get it from the members there and you can print it out and have it. But this is kind of my cheat sheet for me to kind of remember what kind of emails I can send out. So I basically, I broke it down to those episode styles, there's epiphany styles and then there's education styles. Okay. So I'll start here, episodes. So episodes are like, here's like an episode of my life, something that's happening, right? So one might be a controversial email like, oh, I saw this guy, you know, like right now I was recording this, Donald Trump's running for president and he's giving us all sorts of fodder for like controversial, funny emails that can somehow tie back to your thing, right? Controversial email. Story based education, education, how do you say that? Edutaining. So what's the story? What happened to you today? What happened to you when you woke up? What did your kid do last night? What did your wife say? What happened on dinner? What happened this morning when you were going to the office? What, the grocery store? What's that? What did you read online? Like what are the stories that are happening in your life that you can bring in, right? So telling stories and but you're educating, right? So how does this relate to them? How can it give value to them? And what happened today? This is, you know, similar to the story based education, but it's even dumber. This is like, you know, like just a thing. Like, here's something that happened today. By the way, you should go buy my thing, right? So it's just kind of what happened today. So these are two episode styles. They transition more to epiphany styles, right? Whenever I have an idea, like if you guys listen to my marketing, your card, the marketing of your card is probably my closest to consistent Seinfeld emails. We're getting better and better at them soon. Hopefully I'll be a point where I'm perfect like Ben Settle, which hopefully I'll be there someday, not quite yet. But my marketing, your card podcast, I'm a lot closer to that and it's very much a epiphany style, right? Like I have an idea. It's like, hey, guys, I had an idea today. Let me talk. I want to just talk it out. You guys can listen to it. So here's an idea I had. Okay. Or here's an inspiration. Like I saw this person online. I saw this thing and this is how inspirational it was for me. I saw this movie this weekend or any kind of inspiration based things. People love inspirational crap for whatever reason. Okay. Traditionally what will happen when somebody enters our odd responder sequence, they get what we call our soap opera sequence. Some of you guys have gone through that before, that training. You get some kind of soap opera sequence to introduce them to the attractive character and then from there we transition them over to a list that sends out our daily sign filled emails that push people towards whatever our core product or service might be at the time. I do do these all live because for us in the company we've got different agendas. This month I might be focusing on click funnels. Next month I might be focusing more on our high ticket selling. Whatever is the focus that we're publishing that month is where the sign filled emails will be tied back to. That's kind of how we do that. So there you go you guys. That is how you do daily sign filled emails. You've got the blueprint. You've got some examples. Now all you need to do is to go and actually do it. Try it out. I know when I first tried I was nervous. I was scared. I was excited. I think the first one I sent out was actually I went to a meeting with Ben and I've been testing basic ideas and I went to this mastermind group that he was in. He was just talking about it. It was like in tears talking about how the guy treated her and I was like you know what I'm going Ben settle on this guy.
